title: History's Greatest Hits
description: Major moments, people, inventions, and turning points from world history.

questions.prompt: Which ancient civilization built the pyramids at Giza?
questions.hint: The pyramids stand near the Nile River.
questions.explanation: The ancient Egyptians built the pyramids at Giza.

questions.prompt: Who was the first president of the United States?
questions.hint: He also commanded the Continental Army.
questions.explanation: George Washington was the first president of the United States.

questions.prompt: The printing press helped spread books and ideas more widely in Europe.
questions.hint: Think of Gutenberg and movable type.
questions.explanation: Movable type printing made books easier to produce and helped ideas circulate more widely.

questions.prompt: The wall that divided a German city during the Cold War was the Berlin _____.
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall divided East and West Berlin from 1961 until 1989.

questions.prompt: Which empire was ruled by Julius Caesar shortly before it became an empire under Augustus?
questions.explanation: Julius Caesar was a central figure in the late Roman Republic before Augustus became Rome's first emperor.

questions.hint: The storming of the Bastille happened that year.

questions.prompt: Which explorer's 1492 voyage reached the Caribbean while sailing for Spain?
questions.hint: His voyage is often connected with the phrase 'in 1492.'
questions.explanation: Christopher Columbus reached the Caribbean in 1492 while sailing under the Spanish crown.

questions.prompt: The Magna Carta was signed in medieval England.
questions.hint: It is associated with King John in 1215.
questions.explanation: The Magna Carta was agreed to in England in 1215.

questions.prompt: Which war ended with the Treaty of Versailles in 1919?
questions.hint: The treaty followed the armistice of 1918.
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Versailles was one of the major peace treaties ending World War I.

questions.prompt: Who was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize?
questions.hint: She later won a second Nobel Prize in a different science.
questions.explanation: Marie Curie was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize.
